ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "overview" TEXT /* Synopsis or overview of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "budget" INTEGER /* Budget allocated for the movie */,
  "revenue" INTEGER /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the movie */,
  "rating_count" INTEGER /* Number of ratings received */,
  "production_companies" TEXT /* Companies involved in the production */,
  "production_countries" TEXT /* Countries where the movie was produced */,
  "spoken_languages" TEXT /* Languages spoken in the movie */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* Path to the movie poster */,
  "backdrop_path" TEXT /* Path to the movie backdrop image */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline of the movie */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword" TEXT /* Keyword associated with movies */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cast" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast member */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "order" INTEGER /* Order of the cast member in the credits */,
  "gender" INTEGER /* Gender of the actor */,
  "profile_path" TEXT /* Path to the actor's profile image */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity score of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cast_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crew" (
  "crew_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crew member */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the crew member */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the crew member works in */,
  "job" TEXT /* Job title of the crew member */,
  "gender" INTEGER /* Gender of the crew member */,
  "profile_path" TEXT /* Path to the crew member's profile image */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity score of the crew member */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crew_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crew_department FOREIGN KEY ("department") REFERENCES departments ("department_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crew_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crew_job FOREIGN KEY ("job") REFERENCES jobs ("job_title")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_keywords" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user's role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given by the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the movie */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment text */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the comment was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "logo_path" TEXT /* Path to the company's logo */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters location of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the production country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "spoken_languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each spoken language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the spoken language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)
